message:
feat(stdio/vis): port stravis + libbsd disambiguation aliases (bd-twl4f)

Adds three new C entry points to the vis(3) family:

- `stravis(outp, src, flags)` — NetBSD allocating strvis. Allocates
  a `malloc()`-backed buffer of `4 * strlen(src) + 1` bytes (the
  worst-case encoded length), encodes `src` into it, stores the
  pointer in `*outp`, and returns the encoded length. Caller owns
  the buffer and must `free()` it. Returns -1 on NULL inputs or
  allocation failure.

- `strnvis_netbsd(dst, dlen, src, flags)` — direct alias for our
  existing NetBSD-order `strnvis`. libbsd ships this so callers
  can disambiguate against the FreeBSD/glibc `strnvis(dst, src,
  dlen, flags)` argument-order divergence.

- `strnunvis_netbsd(dst, dlen, src)` — direct alias for our
  existing `strnunvis`. Same disambiguation rationale.

Tests: 8 abi integration tests covering: stravis output matches
strvis byte-for-byte, high-bit byte round-trip via strunvis, empty
string yields a valid 1-byte buffer with just NUL, NULL outp/src
return -1; the _netbsd aliases produce identical results to the
unsuffixed entry points and propagate the bounded-overflow -1
contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
